entries.content: I have a linear issue for a new feature about level transitions.  Can you pull that in?

I'll find that Linear issue for you. Let me load the Linear tools first.

Now let me search Linear for the level transitions issue.

None of those match — they're all Jolli backend issues, not the snake game. Let me search more specifically.
its in a different linear project than jolli

Got it — let me look at the projects to find the snake game one.

No "snake" project. Let me list all projects to find the right one.

Let me extract just the project names from that file.

No game project on this page, and these all look like the Jolli team. The snake game might be under a different team entirely. Let me check the teams.

Only one team (Jolli), so the game must be a project I haven't seen yet. Let me get the next page of projects.
it is in a different account at linear.

Ah, that's the blocker then. The Linear connector I have here is authenticated to a single workspace — **jolliai** (your joseph.hart@jolli.ai account). All my Linear tool calls go through that one OAuth connection, so I can't reach issues that live in a different Linear account/workspace. There's no "switch account" tool exposed to me, and I can't re-auth it to the other account myself.

A couple of ways forward — whichever is easier for you:

1. **Just paste the issue here.** Drop the title + description (and any acceptance criteria/comments) and I'll work straight from that. Fastest path.

2. **Point the Linear connector at the other account.** Reconnect/switch the Linear connector to that workspace via `/mcp` (or in your claude.ai connector settings), then tell me and I'll pull the issue by ID/URL.

3. **Give me the issue URL** and I can try to fetch it directly — but Linear issues sit behind auth, so this likely won't return the content unless it's public.

Option 1 is usually the least friction. Want to paste it in?
ok.. can you see the joesthewizardworkspace in linear?

Yes — the connector is now on a different workspace. I can see one team: **Joesworkspace**. Let me find that level transitions issue there.

Found it. Let me pull the full issue details.

Pulled it in. Here's the issue:

## JOE-5 — Create smooth transition from level to level in iron snake mode

> When a user (person or bot) successfully passes a level and a new board is generated it swaps it in and continues. This is a little jarring. I want to create a few-frame transition that morphs the completed board to the next board.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Project** | Snake Game Project |
| **Workspace** | joesthewizardworkspace (team: Joesworkspace) |
| **Status** | Backlog · No priority |
| **Suggested branch** | `josephhart/joe-5-create-smooth-transition-from-level-to-level-in-iron-snake` |
| **Attachments / relations** | none |

**In short:** iron-snake mode currently hard-swaps the board when a level is cleared, which feels jarring. The ask is a short morph/tween animation (a few frames) bridging the completed board → the next generated board.

Want me to start on it? If so I'd:
1. Recall any prior Jolli Memory context on the iron-snake progression work (there's existing head/tail + progression code from recent commits).
2. Look at how level completion / board regeneration works today to find the swap point.
3. Come back with a short plan for the transition before writing code.

Say the word and I'll kick off — I can branch off `main` as `josephhart/joe-5-…` to match Linear's suggested name.
I have a linear issue for a new feature about level transitions.  Can you pull that in?

I'll help pull in that Linear issue. Let me load the Linear tools and recall prior context for this branch per the project guidelines.

Now let me recall branch context and find the Linear issue in parallel.

Found it — **JOE-5: "Create smooth transition from level to level in iron snake mode."** Let me get the full issue and recall the relevant progression/board-shape context, since this builds directly on that work.

Let me read the recall detail and the board-shape context to ground the transition work in existing decisions.

The JSON is on one line. Let me extract the key fields with jq.

Excellent context. The Iron Snake progression system (levels, board regeneration, snake shape preservation) lives in `src/main.ts` and `src/iron-snake.ts`. Now let me explore the actual code — how the board swap happens on level-up, and how rendering/animation is driven — so I can design the morph transition.

Both Explore agents are running in parallel. I'll wait for their findings before designing the transition.

I've loaded the plan-mode tools. Now waiting for the two Explore agents to report back before designing the transition approach.

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>I have a complete picture of the level-advancement and game-loop mechanics. All logic lives in the `SnakeGame` class in `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/main.ts`, with board-shape generation in `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/src/iron-snake.ts`. Here is the map.

## 1. Level-up trigger & swap

**Trigger chain (Iron Snake):** eating food calls `checkGridExpansion()`, which routes to `checkLevelAdvance()`, which fires `advanceLevel()` when the cumulative score meets the goal.

- Food-eaten call sites: `main.ts:1344` (single-player, inside `updateSinglePlayer`) and `main.ts:1444` (two-player, inside `updateTwoPlayer`), both invoking `this.checkGridExpansion()`.
- `checkGridExpansion()` at `main.ts:1947` — in Iron Snake mode delegates to level advance; otherwise uses the classic fill-based grid doubling:
```
if (this.ironSnakeMode) { this.checkLevelAdvance(); return }
...
if (fillPercentage >= 0.25) { this.expandGrid() }
```
- `checkLevelAdvance()` at `main.ts:2076`:
```
const combined = this.score + (this.isTwoSnakeMode() ? this.score2 : 0)
if (combined >= this.levelGoal) { this.advanceLevel() }
```
- The cumulative goal formula: `ironSnakeGoalForLevel` at `main.ts:47` — `(5 * level * (level + 1)) / 2` (5, 15, 30, 50, ...).

**The swap itself — `advanceLevel()` at `main.ts:2083`.** This is the instant, jarring transition. In order it changes:
- `this.level++` — `main.ts:2092`
- **Board size / grid dimensions:** `this.gridSize = baseGrid + extra` inside the retry loop — `main.ts:2096-2099` (base from `ironSnakeGridSizeForLevel(this.level)`).
- **Board mask/shape + area:** `this.regenerateIronSnakeBoard()` — `main.ts:2100` (sets `boardMask` and `boardArea`).
- **Snake position/shape:** `this.reseatKeepingShape(shape1, dir1, shape2, dir2)` — `main.ts:2101` (see section 2).
- **Level goal:** `this.levelGoal = ironSnakeGoalForLevel(this.level)` — `main.ts:2119`.
- **Game speed:** `this.gameSpeed = Math.max(IRON_SNAKE_MIN_SPEED, Math.round(this.baseSpeed * Math.pow(0.9, this.level - 1)))` — `main.ts:2122` (gentle 0.9^n ramp, floored at 60 ms; `baseSpeed`=200).
- **Canvas / iso projection:** `this.updateCanvasSize()` — `main.ts:2123` (rebuilds `basis*`, `isoCache`, canvas dims for the new `gridSize`).
- **Loop restart:** `clearInterval` then `setInterval(..., this.gameSpeed)` — `main.ts:2125-2126`.
- **Food + HUD + render:** `spawnFood()` / `updateUI()` / `draw()` — `main.ts:2128-2130`.

**HUD updates** happen in `updateUI()` at `main.ts:2174`: `#level` (2181), `#grid-size` (2182), and the Iron Snake goal readout `#iron-snake-goal` showing remaining points (2189-2193).

The classic (non-Iron) analog is `expandGrid()` at `main.ts:1964` — doubles `gridSize`, `level++`, halves `gameSpeed`, restarts the interval.

## 2. Snake shape preservation

The snake's geometry is carried across levels as **head-relative offsets**, captured at the top of `advanceLevel()` before the board regenerates — `main.ts:2087-2090`:
```
const shape1 = this.snake.map(s => ({ x: s.x - this.snake[0].x, y: s.y - this.snake[0].y }))
const dir1 = this.direction
const shape2 = two ? this.snake2.map(...) : null
```

Placement pipeline:
- `reseatKeepingShape(shape1, dir1, shape2, dir2)` at `main.ts:2037` — seats snake(s) on the new board near preferred anchors (`N/2,N/2` single; `0.25N`/`0.75N` two-player), resets loop memory + `movesSinceFood`, assigns `snake`/`snakeSet`/`direction`/`nextDirection`. Returns false if it can't fit.
- Per snake it calls `placeSnakeKeepingShape` at `main.ts:1793`, a two-tier strategy:
  1. **Rigid (translate + rotate)** — `placeRigid` at `main.ts:1671`: translates the head to an anchor near `preferred` (candidates sorted by Manhattan distance), tries orientations k=0..3 (identity first, then 90/180/270°) via `rotateOffset` (`main.ts:1644`) and `rotateDirection` (`main.ts:1661`). A placement is valid only if every segment is on-board and unoccupied; prefers a head with forward `clearance>=3`, falls back to `>=1`.
  2. **Warnsdorff self-avoiding fallback** — `layoutBody` at `main.ts:1718`: when the exact shape won't seat, keeps only the *length*, laying a self-avoiding walk from a start cell (`findStartCell`, `main.ts:1617`), sorting candidate steps by fewest free onward neighbours (Warnsdorff, `main.ts:1766-1775`), tie-broken toward going straight. Returns `Position[]` or null.

Retry/fallback ladder in `advanceLevel` — `main.ts:2098-2114`: try `baseGrid + extra` for extra 0..3 (bigger box each retry); then a full rectangle (`boardMask = null`); last-resort `repositionSnakes()` (resets to length 1). `reseatKeepingShape` returns bodies; the callers write them into `this.snake`/`this.snake2` with fresh sets and directions.

Board-size helper: `ironSnakeGridSizeForLevel` at `main.ts:1813` sizes the box so the snake's end-of-level length hits `IRON_SNAKE_GOAL_FILL` (0.4) occupancy, via `ironSnakeGridSizeForArea` in `iron-snake.ts:201`. Monotonic, floored at `max(IRON_SNAKE_MIN_SIZE=12, initialGridSize)`.

## 3. Game loop / timing

- **Driver: `setInterval`.** The one logic loop is `this.gameLoop = window.setInterval(() => this.update(), this.gameSpeed)`. `gameLoop` is `number | null` (`main.ts:112`). Created/recreated at:
  - `startLoopIfNeeded()` — `main.ts:1188-1193`
  - first directional keypress (single-player lazy start) — `main.ts:1102`
  - `expandGrid()` — `main.ts:1972`
  - `advanceLevel()` — `main.ts:2125-2126`
- **Tick body:** `update()` at `main.ts:1300` early-returns if `isPaused || isGameOver`, else dispatches to `updateSinglePlayer` (`main.ts:1310`) or `updateTwoPlayer` (`main.ts:1365`).
- **Cadence:** `gameSpeed` ms/tick. `baseSpeed`=200 (`main.ts:110-111`). Iron Snake ramps 200 → floor 60 ms (`IRON_SNAKE_MIN_SPEED`, `main.ts:25`); classic halves per expand.
- **Render vs logic:** There is **no separate render loop for gameplay** — `draw()` is called at the end of each tick (`main.ts:1361`, `main.ts:1475`) and on discrete events (rotate/overhead/resize). The only `requestAnimationFrame` loop is `autoRotateStep` (`main.ts:661-673`, started by `startAutoRotate` at `main.ts:619`), which spins the isometric camera and calls `updateCanvasSize()` + `draw()` independently of the logic tick — a working precedent for a render-only animation driver that does not advance game state.
- **Pausing logic ticks:** two existing mechanisms — the `isPaused` flag gate at the top of `update()` (`main.ts:1301`, toggled by `togglePause` `main.ts:1113`; note the interval keeps firing but does nothing), and fully tearing down the interval via `if (this.gameLoop) clearInterval(this.gameLoop); this.gameLoop = null` (pattern used at `main.ts:832-833`, `958-959`, `1279-1280`, `2125`). An RAF-based animation could run while the interval is cleared/paused, mirroring `autoRotateStep`.

## 4. Game state shape

Not module-level globals — all instance fields on class `SnakeGame` (declared `main.ts:76`, constructed at bottom of file). Key fields:

- **Board mask/shape:** `boardMask: Uint8Array | null` — `main.ts:106` (flat `gridSize*gridSize`, 1=playable, 0=wall; null=full rectangle).
- **Board area / capacity:** `boardArea: number` — `main.ts:107`.
- **Grid dimensions:** `gridSize: number` — `main.ts:99`; `initialGridSize: number` — `main.ts:100`.
- **Snake 1:** `snake: Position[]` — `main.ts:84`; `snakeSet: Set<string>` — `main.ts:85`; `direction`/`nextDirection` — `main.ts:86-87`; `score` — `main.ts:88`.
- **Snake 2:** `snake2` / `snakeSet2` / `direction2` / `nextDirection2` / `score2` — `main.ts:91-95`.
- **Food:** `food: Position` — `main.ts:98`.
- **Level/goal/speed:** `levelGoal` — `main.ts:108`; `level` — `main.ts:109`; `gameSpeed` — `main.ts:110`; `baseSpeed` — `main.ts:111`.
- **Mode/loop flags:** `ironSnakeMode` — `main.ts:105`; `gameLoop` — `main.ts:112`; `isPaused` — `main.ts:113`; `isGameOver` — `main.ts:114`; `gameStarted` — `main.ts:115`.
- **Iso render cache** (rebuilt on size change, would need re-morphing during a transition): `basisXx/Xy/Yx/Yy`, `isoCache`, `isoOriginX/Y` — `main.ts:138-148`.
- **Module-level** (not per-game): tuning constants and `ironSnakeGoalForLevel` at `main.ts:20-55`; the two exported generators in `iron-snake.ts` (`generateIronSnakeBoard` `iron-snake.ts:152`, `ironSnakeGridSizeForArea` `iron-snake.ts:201`).

Note: `advanceLevel` is the single point where an old board and new board coexist conceptually (old snake shape captured at `main.ts:2087-2090`, new board built at `main.ts:2099-2101`), so both the source geometry (head-relative offsets + gridSize/mask before) and the target geometry (after `reseatKeepingShape`) are available there — relevant for any morph that needs start and end states. This is a mapping only, per your request; no solution proposed.</result>

First Explore agent done — clear map of the swap point (`advanceLevel()` at `main.ts:2083`) and a strong precedent: `autoRotateStep` already runs an RAF render-only loop (`main.ts:661-673`) that animates without advancing game logic. Waiting on the rendering-pipeline agent now.

<summary>Agent "Explore rendering pipeline" finished</summary>

<result>I have everything needed to map the rendering pipeline. Here is the trace.

## 1. Canvas / draw surface

**Canvas 2D context** (not WebGL, not DOM). Two independent canvases:
- Game board: `<canvas id="canvas">` inside `.canvas-wrap` — `index.html:91`. Context grabbed at `src/main.ts:198` `this.ctx = this.canvas.getContext('2d')!`.
- Menu preview: `<canvas id="menu-demo-canvas">` — `index.html:13`, driven by a separate `MenuDemo` class in `src/menu-demo.ts` (its own duplicate iso pipeline).

**Dimensions** computed in `updateCanvasSize()` (`src/main.ts:238`). `getIsoWidth()` (`main.ts:221`) = `window.innerWidth - 70` clamped to `[ISO_MIN_WIDTH=300, ISO_MAX_WIDTH=1200]` (`main.ts:20-21`). Then (`main.ts:274-278`):
```
this.canvas.width  = isoWidth + padding*2          // padding = 20
this.canvas.height = isoWidth*perspectiveRatio + padding*2 + baseBlockHeight
```
Resize re-runs `updateCanvasSize()` + `draw()` (`main.ts:204`).

## 2. Per-frame draw

Single frame = `private draw()` at **`src/main.ts:677`** (signature `private draw()`, no args — reads instance state). Order:

1. Clear: `ctx.fillStyle='#1a1a1a'; fillRect(...)` — `main.ts:679-680`
2. `drawGroundPlane()` (`main.ts:302`) — checkerboard floor; skips off-board cells via mask (`main.ts:310`); batched into two `Path2D` filled `#2a2a2a`/`#222222` (`main.ts:326-329`)
3. `drawGridLines()` (`main.ts:332`) — faint `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)` lattice; early-returns if tile <5px (`main.ts:334-335`)
4. `drawGroundBorder()` (`main.ts:385`) — `rgba(255,255,255,0.15)` outline; per-cell edge tracing when masked
5. Collect renderable `objects[]` (snake head/body/tail, snake2, food, death cells) — `main.ts:687-722`
6. Painter's-algorithm depth sort by projected Y (`main.ts:727-729`)
7. Shadows: `drawBlockShadow()` per object (`main.ts:732-734`; fn at `main.ts:449`)
8. Compute per-frame colors (hunger tint + final-fruit) — `main.ts:738-751`
9. Draw blocks via switch (`main.ts:754-781`) dispatching to the drawers below
10. Off-grid classic-wall death sliver drawn last (`main.ts:786-791`)

Key sub-functions:
- `drawPrism(c, bh, topColor, rightColor, leftColor)` — **`main.ts:484`**. Extrudes a prism from 4 clockwise ground corners; classifies side faces front/back by screen-X normal, paints back→front→top (`main.ts:497-532`).
- `drawBlock(gx, gy, top, right, left, heightScale=1)` — `main.ts:467`. Projects the 4 inset cell corners (inset 0.05) and calls `drawPrism`.
- `drawSnakeHead(gx, gy, colors, dir)` — `main.ts:542`. Taller block (`HEAD_HEIGHT_SCALE=1.6`, `main.ts:536`) + eyes on the leading face (skipped if face hidden or overhead).
- `drawSnakeTail(gx, gy, colors, awayDir)` — `main.ts:594`. Tapered prism (`TAIL_HEIGHT_SCALE=0.7`, `main.ts:583`).
- `drawBlockShadow(gx, gy)` — `main.ts:449`. Flat `rgba(0,0,0,0.3)` diamond.

**HUD is DOM, not canvas** — score/level/goal spans updated in `updateUI()` (`main.ts:2174`); starvation/death banners are HTML overlays (`index.html:92-93`).

Note the color convention throughout: every block/prism takes a `[topColor, rightColor, leftColor]` 3-tuple (top face, right-lit face, left-shaded face).

## 3. Board geometry → screen

**Projection**: `toIso(gridX, gridY)` — **`main.ts:227`**. Applies a 2×2 basis matrix then a perspective divide:
```
rawX = gridX*basisXx + gridY*basisYx
rawY = gridX*basisXy + gridY*basisYy
d = rawMaxY - rawY;  scale = focalLength/(focalLength+d)   // Infinity => 1 (no perspective)
```
returns screen `{x,y}` offset by `isoOriginX/Y`. Lattice points are precomputed into `isoCache[gy][gx]` (`main.ts:292-299`).

**Camera / origin / scale** — all set in `updateCanvasSize()` (`main.ts:238-300`):
- `isoAngle = Math.PI/3` (`main.ts:135`), `perspectiveRatio = 0.5` (`main.ts:136`), `perspectiveStrength = 0.4` (`main.ts:142`)
- `scale = isoWidth / (gridSize*Math.SQRT2)` (`main.ts:245`)
- basis vectors from `cosA/sinA * scale (* pr)` (`main.ts:247-250`)
- `baseBlockHeight = overheadView ? 0 : scale*pr*SQRT2*0.6` (`main.ts:255`)
- `focalLength` from depth range / `perspectiveStrength` (`main.ts:271`)
- `isoOriginX/Y` anchor grid center to canvas center so rotation pivots (`main.ts:289-290`)
- Per-cell perspective height: `getBlockHeight(gx,gy)` (`main.ts:441`)

**Board mask / shape**: `boardMask: Uint8Array | null` flat `gridSize*gridSize`, 1=playable (`main.ts:106`); `onBoard(x,y)` at `main.ts:1583`. Affects rendering in three places: floor skips off-board cells (`main.ts:310`); grid lines and border have masked branches that draw only edges bordering on-board cells / off-board neighbours (`main.ts:356-380`, `main.ts:406-438`). Generated by `generateIronSnakeBoard(gridSize)` (`src/iron-snake.ts:152`, returns `{mask, area}`).

**Overhead toggle**: `toggleOverheadView()` — **`main.ts:641`**. Saves iso settings, then sets `isoAngle=0, perspectiveRatio=1, perspectiveStrength=0` (flat, axis-aligned, no perspective); restores on toggle-off (`main.ts:643-656`). Because `baseBlockHeight=0` when overhead (`main.ts:255`), blocks flatten to squares and head eyes are suppressed (`main.ts:543,547`). Auto-rotation is also gated off while overhead (`main.ts:664`).

## 4. Existing animation / tween infrastructure

- **`lerpHex(from, to, t)`** — **`main.ts:1932`**: linear RGB interpolation between two `#rrggbb` colors. This is the one reusable color-tween primitive.
- **`hungerFactor(movesSinceFood)`** — `main.ts:1916`: returns a normalized 0→1 ramp (starts at `WARN_FRACTION`). Consumed by the `tint()` closure that `lerpHex`-blends snake colors toward the warning palette each frame (`main.ts:738-746`).
- **RAF frame-loop with time delta** — `autoRotateStep(timestamp)` — `main.ts:661`: computes `dt`, advances `isoAngle`, calls `updateCanvasSize()`+`draw()`, re-schedules via `requestAnimationFrame` (`main.ts:664-672`); started/stopped at `main.ts:619-632`. The richest example is `MenuDemo.frame(timestamp)` — `src/menu-demo.ts:93`: same pattern with a `moveAccumulator` (fixed-step) and a `deathPauseRemaining` countdown (`menu-demo.ts:99-117`). These are the existing "advance state over elapsed time, redraw" loops to mirror.
- **Discrete color cue (not a tween)** — `IRON_SNAKE_FINAL_FRUIT_COLORS` (`main.ts:51-55`) swaps the fruit palette for the last 3 fruits of a level (`main.ts:748-751`).
- **CSS keyframe animations** (DOM overlays only, not canvas): `starve-pulse` (`src/style.css:195`), `death-pulse` (`style.css:222`), `expand-panel-fade` (`style.css:386`); button `transition: all 0.25s` (`style.css:138`).

No generic easing/tween library exists — the toolkit is `lerpHex` + the RAF-`dt` loop pattern.

The board-swap seam JOE-5 targets is **`advanceLevel()` — `main.ts:2083`**: it captures each snake's shape, regenerates `boardMask` for the new level, reseats snakes, then calls `updateCanvasSize()`+`draw()` synchronously (`main.ts:2123-2130`) — i.e. today the old board is replaced by the new one in a single frame with no interpolation. (`checkLevelAdvance()` `main.ts:2076` triggers it.)

## 5. Colors / theming

All hardcoded per-role, defined inline in `draw()` unless noted:
- Background `#1a1a1a` (`main.ts:679`); floor `#2a2a2a`/`#222222` (`main.ts:326-329`); grid lines `rgba(255,255,255,0.06)`; border `rgba(255,255,255,0.15)`; shadow `rgba(0,0,0,0.3)` (`main.ts:463`).
- Snake 1: head `['#4ade80','#22c55e','#16a34a']`, body `['#22c55e','#16a34a','#15803d']` (`main.ts:743-744`).
- Snake 2: head `['#60a5fa','#3b82f6','#2563eb']`, body `['#3b82f6','#2563eb','#1d4ed8']` (`main.ts:745-746`).
- Food default `['#ef4444','#dc2626','#b91c1c']` (`main.ts:751`).
- Death/impact `['#facc15','#eab308','#a16207']` (`main.ts:778,789`).
- Hunger palettes `HUNGER_HEAD_COLORS` / `HUNGER_BODY_COLORS` (`main.ts:43-44`); final-fruit palettes `main.ts:51-55`.

**Per-level color variation: none.** Board and snake colors are constant across levels. The only dynamic recoloring is (a) the hunger tint (by starvation, via `lerpHex`) and (b) the final-3-fruit cue (by fruits-remaining). If JOE-5 wants per-level board coloring it would be introducing that concept, not reusing an existing one.</result>

Both agents delivered a complete map. Now let me read the critical code sections directly to ground the design — the swap point, the RAF precedent, the render entry, and canvas sizing.

Let me read the canvas-sizing routine, the loop-start helper, and check for any existing reduced-motion handling to match conventions.

I now have everything I need: the swap happens synchronously in `advanceLevel()` (`main.ts:2083`), the game runs on a `setInterval` logic tick with `draw()` called per-tick, and there's a clean RAF render-only precedent (`autoRotateStep`) plus a `lerpHex` color-tween and a `drawImage`-able canvas. No reduced-motion handling exists yet.

My recommended approach: snapshot the old board before mutating, apply the existing level-advance mutations, snapshot the new board, then run a short RAF loop (mirroring `autoRotateStep`) that cross-composites the two snapshots — pausing the logic interval for the ~450ms it runs. Two genuine product forks remain, so let me confirm those before finalizing.

No response — I'll proceed with my recommended defaults (**Dissolve + zoom**, **Iron Snake only** — matching the issue), and note in the plan that the style is easily swappable. Let me write the plan file.

I've pulled in **JOE-5** and written a plan. Summary of what I found and propose:

**The problem:** `advanceLevel()` (`src/main.ts:2083`) swaps the whole board in one synchronous frame — new mask, size, reseated snake, resized canvas — which is the jarring cut the issue describes.

**The approach:** snapshot the old board, apply the existing mutations to reach the new board, snapshot that, then run a short (~450 ms) `requestAnimationFrame` loop that cross-dissolves + gently zooms from old→new. This reuses `draw()` untouched, mirrors the existing `autoRotateStep` RAF precedent, pauses the logic tick for the duration, and falls back to today's instant swap under `prefers-reduced-motion`. All changes land in `src/main.ts`.

Two things I decided without you (you were away) — both trivially reversible:

- **Scope: Iron Snake only** (matches the issue; classic-mode `expandGrid` could reuse the helper later)

That confirms the clean lever: all three board-drawing functions (`drawGroundPlane` reads `this.boardMask` directly at line 310; `drawGridLines` and `drawGroundBorder` read it via `onBoard`) key entirely off `this.boardMask`. So I can animate the reshape by swapping in an interpolated "display mask" each frame and calling the existing `draw()` — the outline, grid lines, and floor all reshape cell-by-cell for free, with zero changes to the render functions. Let me rewrite the plan around a true geometric morph.

Reworked the plan around a **true geometric morph**. The clean insight: the board's shape is entirely defined by one field (`boardMask`), and all three board-drawing functions read only that field. So I animate the reshape by feeding `draw()` an interpolated "display mask" each frame — the **outline, grid lines, and floor all reshape cell-by-cell for free**, no render-function changes.

How it works:
- Build a `startDisplay` mask (old board's cells stamped into the new, larger grid, with the snake + food unioned in so nothing floats) and a `targetDisplay` mask (the new level's shape).
- Reveal the cells that differ, ordered as an **inside-out wave** — the arena grows outward and old-only cells peel away as the wave passes — over ~600 ms via a `requestAnimationFrame` loop (same pattern as the existing `autoRotateStep`).
- Logic ticks are paused for the duration; `prefers-reduced-motion` and re-entrancy fall back to the instant swap. Scoped to Iron Snake mode. All in `src/main.ts`.

One honest caveat noted in the plan: cell scale jumps to the new `gridSize` at the morph's first frame (a subtle shrink, since per-level growth is small); animating the scale too is possible later but adds real complexity. The board *shape* morphs smoothly regardless.

**Verdict: PASS**

**Claim:** On a level advance in Iron Snake mode, the playable board should reshape cell-by-cell from the old shape into the new one over ~600ms (instead of an instant swap), then normal play resumes; `prefers-reduced-motion` falls back to the instant swap. Diff (186 insertions in `src/main.ts`) matches this — `advanceLevel` now snapshots the old mask, applies mutations via `applyLevelAdvance`, and drives an RAF morph loop, with a reduced-motion short-circuit.

5. ✅ **Two consecutive transitions** (`1→2` and `2→3`) both morphed cleanly — no double-fire, no dead loop.

I have seen some levels go by that didn't change the level shape

Good catch — that reveals a real flaw in my grow approach. Here's why: `growIronSnakeBoard` seeds from the *old board* and stops once it hits the target fill. When the grid size doesn't increase (which is common — it's floored at 12 for many early levels), the old board is *already* at target fill, so zero new rooms get added and the shape is identical. The board only changed when the grid actually grew.

The fix: seed the fresh board from the **snake itself** (a clear room around the snake's bounding box), then grow random rooms outward every level. That regenerates a genuinely new shape each level while still keeping the snake in place and always connected/valid.

Fixed. The root cause was in how I grew the board: `growIronSnakeBoard` seeded from the **old board** and stopped at the target fill. When the grid size didn't increase (common — it's floored at 12 for many early levels), the old board was already at target fill, so no new rooms were added and the shape came out identical. **The fix:** seed the fresh board from the **snake itself** instead of the old board. `growIronSnakeBoard` now lays a clear room around the snake's bounding box (keeping every snake cell playable, connected, and with room to move), then grows a fresh set of random rooms outward until target fill. Because the seed is small and the rooms are re-rolled each time, the shape genuinely changes every level — while the snake still never moves.
